items.q: Did Hoover do nothing about the Depression?
items.correct: Hoover acted more than his reputation suggests — the Reconstruction Finance Corporation lent to banks and businesses in 1932 — but he opposed direct federal relief, so he seemed uncaring.
items.misconception: Hoover did absolutely nothing while Americans starved.
items.random_wrong: Hoover launched the first New Deal in 1930.
items.why: Students absorb the 'do-nothing' caricature; the better point is that his measures were too limited and too late, not absent.

items.q: Did everyone share in the 1920s boom?
items.correct: No — farmers, Black Americans and workers in older industries like coal and textiles saw little benefit, and wealth remained very unevenly spread.
items.misconception: All Americans grew richer during the Roaring Twenties.
items.random_wrong: Only farmers benefited from the 1920s boom.
items.why: Students generalise from consumer-boom imagery; the excluded groups explain why demand was fragile when the Crash came.

items.q: Why did so many American banks fail between 1929 and 1933?
items.correct: Banks had lent and speculated heavily, and panicking customers staged bank runs to withdraw deposits; with no federal deposit insurance until 1933, failures cascaded.
items.misconception: The government deliberately closed the banks to punish speculators.
items.random_wrong: Banks failed because the US dollar was abolished in 1930.
items.why: Students reverse cause and effect with the 1933 'bank holiday'; runs by depositors, not punishment, destroyed the banks.

items.q: What did Hoover mean by 'rugged individualism'?
items.correct: People should overcome hardship through self-reliance rather than depend on government handouts
items.misconception: The government should employ everyone directly
items.why: The answer examiners want is: People should overcome hardship through self-reliance rather than depend on government handouts. “The government should employ everyone directly” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which body declared parts of the New Deal, including the NRA and AAA, unconstitutional?
items.why: It's The Supreme Court. “The Senate” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which industry, symbolised by the Model T, powered much of the 1920s boom?
items.why: It's The motor industry. “The railway industry” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Why did the USA restrict immigration with quota laws in the 1920s?
items.correct: Fear of communism and prejudice against southern and eastern European immigrants fuelled demands for restriction
items.misconception: There was no more land available for settlement
items.random_wrong: The League of Nations ordered the restrictions
items.why: It's Fear of communism and prejudice against southern and eastern European immigrants fuelled demands for restriction. “There was no more land available for settlement” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Franklin D. Roosevelt first elected president?
items.why: The real figure is 1932 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
